title: Consumer Lifecycle, Cancellation, Readiness, and Graceful Shutdown · Compiler Case: Consumer Tag Generation
summary: A consumer reports ready before consume-ok, ignores broker cancellation, closes the connection while handlers run, and assumes cancel immediately stops every in-flight delivery. Planned artifact: Add and verify the consumer tag generation increment in consumer registration, readiness, and shutdown state machine. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Track consumer tags and channel generations so stale cancellation, delivery, and close events cannot mutate current state.
Report ready only after connection, topology, channel, prefetch, consume registration, and required dependencies are ready.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Predict Consumer Tag Generation · Theory Consumer Tag Generation
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory messaging team handles case 7050 in a accessible municipal task intake with two worker pools. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to sequence readiness, cancellation, in-flight draining, deadline, and channel close; prove topology rollback before deleting the old binding; then remove the dead-letter binding and expose the resulting retention risk. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
